tu.prop: In particular, they have played a major role in the formation of calciumcarbonates (i.e., calcification), which has been considered to be an extracellular process.

tuv.seg: An Early-Branching MicrobialiteCyanobacterium FormsIntracellular Carbonates<g id="1">Estelle Couradeau,</g><g id="2">1,2,3 </g><g id="3">Karim Benzerara,</g><g id="4">1</g><g id="5">* Emmanuelle Gérard,</g><g id="6">2 </g><g id="7">David Moreira,</g><g id="8">3</g><g id="9">Sylvain Bernard,</g><g id="10">4 </g><g id="11">Gordon E. Brown Jr.,</g><g id="12">5,6 </g><g id="13">Purificación López-García</g><g id="14">3</g><bx id="15"/>Cyanobacteria have affected major geochemical cycles (carbon, nitrogen, and oxygen) on Earth forbillions of years.

Une cyanobactérie microbialite à ramification précoce forme des carbonates intracellulaires<g id="1">Estelle Couradeau,</g><g id="2">1,2,3</g><g id="3"> Karim Benzerara,</g><g id="4">1</g><g id="5">* Emmanuelle Gérard,</g><g id="6">2 </g><g id="7">David Moreira,</g><g id="8">3</g><g id="9">Sylvain Bernard,</g><g id="10">4 </g><g id="11">Gordon E. Brown Jr.,</g><g id="12">5,6</g><g id="13">Purificación López-García</g><g id="14">3</g> <bx id="15"/>Les cyanobactéries influencent les principaux cycles géochimiques (carbone, azote et oxygène) sur Terre depuis des milliards d'années.

tu.prop: An Early-Branching MicrobialiteCyanobacterium FormsIntracellular CarbonatesEstelle Couradeau,1,2,3 Karim Benzerara,1* Emmanuelle Gérard,2 David Moreira,3Sylvain Bernard,4 Gordon E. Brown Jr.,5,6 Purificación López-García3Cyanobacteria have affected major geochemical cycles (carbon, nitrogen, and oxygen) on Earth forbillions of years.

Weidentified a cyanobacterium in modern microbialites in Lake Alchichica (Mexico) that formsintracellular amorphous calcium-magnesium-strontium-barium carbonate inclusions about 270nanometers in average diameter, revealing an unexplored pathway for calcification.

tuv.seg: In particular, they have played a major role in the formation of calciumcarbonates (i.e., calcification), which has been considered to be an extracellular process.

Plus particulièrement, ces cyanobactéries jouent un rôle fondamental dans la formation des carbonates de calcium (c'est-à-dire dans la calcification), ce qui est considéré comme un processus extracellulaire.

tu.prop: In particular, they have played a major role in the formation of calciumcarbonates (i.e., calcification), which has been considered to be an extracellular process.
Phylogeneticanalyses place this cyanobacterium within the deeply divergent order Gloeobacterales.

tuv.seg: Weidentified a cyanobacterium in modern microbialites in Lake Alchichica (Mexico) that formsintracellular amorphous calcium-magnesium-strontium-barium carbonate inclusions about 270nanometers in average diameter, revealing an unexplored pathway for calcification.

Nous avons identifié une cyanobactérie dans les microbialites modernes de la lagune Alchichica (au Mexique) qui forme des inclusions de carbonate amorphe de calcium-magnésium-strontium-baryum intracellulaires d'un diamètre moyen d'environ 270 nanomètres, révélant ainsi une voie de calcification inexplorée.

tu.prop: Weidentified a cyanobacterium in modern microbialites in Lake Alchichica (Mexico) that formsintracellular amorphous calcium-magnesium-strontium-barium carbonate inclusions about 270nanometers in average diameter, revealing an unexplored pathway for calcification.
Thechemical composition and structure of the intracellular precipitates suggest some level of cellularcontrol on the biomineralization process.

tuv.seg: Phylogeneticanalyses place this cyanobacterium within the deeply divergent order Gloeobacterales.
Les analyses phylogénétiques placent cette cyanobactérie dans l'ordre profondément divergent des Gloeobactérales.

tu.prop: Phylogeneticanalyses place this cyanobacterium within the deeply divergent order Gloeobacterales.

This discovery expands the diversity of organismscapable of forming amorphous calcium carbonates.Cyanobacteria constitute a phylogeneticallydiverse group of bacteria that can carryout oxygenic photosynthesis (1).

tuv.seg: Thechemical composition and structure of the intracellular precipitates suggest some level of cellularcontrol on the biomineralization process.
La composition chimique et la structure des précipités intracellulaires suggèrent un certain niveau de contrôle cellulaire sur le processus de biominéralisation.

tu.prop: Thechemical composition and structure of the intracellular precipitates suggest some level of cellularcontrol on the biomineralization process.

Thesecosmopolitan microorganisms occupy a widearray of terrestrial, marine, and freshwater habitats.They are major modern primary producers,as exemplified by the two marine cyanobacterialgenera Prochloroccocus and Synechoccocus,whichare responsible for 25% of global photosynthesis(2).

tuv.seg: This discovery expands the diversity of organismscapable of forming amorphous calcium carbonates.<ex id="_15"/><g id="16">C</g><bx id="17"/>yanobacteria constitute a phylogeneticallydiverse group of bacteria that can carryout oxygenic photosynthesis (1).

Cette découverte élargit la diversité des organismes capables de former des carbonates de calcium amorphes.<ex id="_15"/> Les <g id="16">C</g><bx id="17"/>yanobactéries constituent un groupe diversifié sur le plan phylogénique de bactéries capables de réaliser la photosynthèse oxygénique (1).

tu.prop: This discovery expands the diversity of organismscapable of forming amorphous calcium carbonates.Cyanobacteria constitute a phylogeneticallydiverse group of bacteria that can carryout oxygenic photosynthesis (1).
In addition to their evolutionary importanceas ancestors of chloroplasts in photosyntheticeukaryotes (1), cyanobacteria have an extensivefossil record.

tuv.seg: Thesecosmopolitan microorganisms occupy a widearray of terrestrial, marine, and freshwater habitats.They are major modern primary producers,as exemplified by the two marine cyanobacterialgenera Prochloroccocus and Synechoccocus,whichare responsible for 25% of global photosynthesis(2).

Ces micro-organismes cosmopolites occupent un large éventail d'habitats terrestres, marins et d'eau douce. Ce sont d'importants producteurs primaires modernes, comme en témoignent les deux genres de cyanobactéries marines Prochloroccocus et Synechoccocus, qui sont responsables de 25 % de la photosynthèse mondiale(2).

tu.prop: Thesecosmopolitan microorganisms occupy a widearray of terrestrial, marine, and freshwater habitats.They are major modern primary producers,as exemplified by the two marine cyanobacterialgenera Prochloroccocus and Synechoccocus,whichare responsible for 25% of global photosynthesis(2).
Calcified cyanobacteria commonlyoccur since the base of the Cambrian, the earliestundisputed occurrence being Girvanella at 700million years ago (3).

tuv.seg: In addition to their evolutionary importanceas ancestors of chloroplasts in photosyntheticeukaryotes (1), cyanobacteria have an extensivefossil record.
Outre leur importance évolutive en tant qu'ancêtres des chloroplastes chez les eucaryotes photosynthétiques (1), les cyanobactéries possèdent un vaste registre fossile.

tu.prop: In addition to their evolutionary importanceas ancestors of chloroplasts in photosyntheticeukaryotes (1), cyanobacteria have an extensivefossil record.
Moreover, the occurrenceof massive fossil stromatolites likely traces theiremergence back to at least 2.7 billion years ago(4).

tuv.seg: Calcified cyanobacteria commonlyoccur since the base of the Cambrian, the earliestundisputed occurrence being Girvanella at 700million years ago (3).
Les cyanobactéries calcifiées apparaissent couramment depuis la base du Cambrien, la première occurrence incontestable étant Girvanella il y a 700 millions d’années (3).

tu.prop: Calcified cyanobacteria commonlyoccur since the base of the Cambrian, the earliestundisputed occurrence being Girvanella at 700million years ago (3).
Since then, they have played a major role inthe carbon cycle by converting CO2 into organiccarbon and carbonates (2) and incidentally enrichingthe atmosphere in oxygen (5).

tuv.seg: Moreover, the occurrenceof massive fossil stromatolites likely traces theiremergence back to at least 2.7 billion years ago(4).
De plus, l'apparition de stromatolithes fossiles massifs remonte probablement à au moins 2,7 milliards d'années (4), prouvant que les cyanobactéries existaient déjà à cette époque.

tu.prop: Moreover, the occurrenceof massive fossil stromatolites likely traces theiremergence back to at least 2.7 billion years ago(4).
Carbonate precipitation by some cyanobacteriaresults from their photosynthetic activity(6, 7).

tuv.seg: Since then, they have played a major role inthe carbon cycle by converting CO<ex id="_17"/><g id="18">2 </g><bx id="19"/>into organiccarbon and carbonates (2) and incidentally enrichingthe atmosphere in oxygen (5).

Depuis lors, les cyanobactéries jouent un rôle majeur dans le cycle du carbone en convertissant le CO<ex id="_17"/><g id="18">2</g><bx id="19"/>en carbone organique et en carbonates (2) et en enrichissant incidemment l'atmosphère en oxygène (5).

tu.prop: Since then, they have played a major role inthe carbon cycle by converting CO2 into organiccarbon and carbonates (2) and incidentally enrichingthe atmosphere in oxygen (5).

Photosynthesis locally increases the concentrationof CO32– through the disproportionationof bicarbonate to carbonate and CO2,which is fixed by ribulose-1,5-bisphosphatecarboxylase-oxygenase (RuBisCO).

tuv.seg: Carbonate precipitation by some cyanobacteriaresults from their photosynthetic activity(6, 7).
La précipitation de carbonates par certaines cyanobactéries résulte de leur activité photosynthétique (6, 7).

tu.prop: Carbonate precipitation by some cyanobacteriaresults from their photosynthetic activity(6, 7).
The exportof alkalinity from the intracellular to the extracellularmedium involves a mechanism that isstill poorly documented.

tuv.seg: Photosynthesis locally increases the concentrationof CO<ex id="_19"/><g id="20">32– </g><g id="21">through the disproportionationof bicarbonate to carbonate and CO</g><g id="22">2</g><bx id="23"/>,which is fixed by ribulose-1,5-bisphosphatecarboxylase-oxygenase (RuBisCO).

La photosynthèse augmente localement la concentration de CO<ex id="_19"/><g id="20">32-</g><g id="21">par la dismutation du bicarbonate en carbonate et en CO</g><g id="22">2</g><bx id="23"/>. Ce dernier est fixé par la ribulose-1,5-bisphosphate carboxylase-oxygénase (RuBisCO).

tu.prop: Photosynthesis locally increases the concentrationof CO32– through the disproportionationof bicarbonate to carbonate and CO2,which is fixed by ribulose-1,5-bisphosphatecarboxylase-oxygenase (RuBisCO).

This export processraises the saturation index of the surroundingmicroenvironment for carbonate minerals, leadingto mineral precipitation if free cations (e.g.,Ca2+) and nucleation sites are available (6).

tuv.seg: The exportof alkalinity from the intracellular to the extracellularmedium involves a mechanism that isstill poorly documented.
L’export de l’alcalinité du milieu intracellulaire vers le milieu extracellulaire implique un mécanisme encore peu étudié.

tu.prop: The exportof alkalinity from the intracellular to the extracellularmedium involves a mechanism that isstill poorly documented.
Inall cases described so far [e.g., (2, 8)], carbonatemineral precipitation by cyanobacteria isextracellular.

tuv.seg: This export processraises the saturation index of the surroundingmicroenvironment for carbonate minerals, leadingto mineral precipitation if free cations (e.g.,Ca<ex id="_23"/><g id="24">2+</g><bx id="25"/>) and nucleation sites are available (6).

Ce processus d’export augmente l’indice de saturation du micro-milieu environnant pour les minéraux carbonatés, ce qui conduit à la précipitation minérale si des cations libres (par exemple, Ca<ex id="_23"/><g id="24">2+</g><bx id="25"/>) et des sites de nucléation sont disponibles (6).

tu.prop: This export processraises the saturation index of the surroundingmicroenvironment for carbonate minerals, leadingto mineral precipitation if free cations (e.g.,Ca2+) and nucleation sites are available (6).
As a result, the chemical compositionof precipitates depends essentially onthe surrounding environmental conditions, withlittle control by the cell (9).

tuv.seg: Inall cases described so far [e.g., (2, 8)], carbonatemineral precipitation by cyanobacteria isextracellular.
Dans tous les cas décrits jusqu'à présent [par exemple (2, 8)], la précipitation des minéraux carbonatés par les cyanobactéries est extracellulaire.

tu.prop: Inall cases described so far [e.g., (2, 8)], carbonatemineral precipitation by cyanobacteria isextracellular.
Calcification efficiencyvaries among different species as a functionof extracellular surface properties, includingexopolymer composition (10) or varying physiologicalstates of the cell (11, 12).

tuv.seg: As a result, the chemical compositionof precipitates depends essentially onthe surrounding environmental conditions, withlittle control by the cell (9).
Par conséquent, la composition chimique des précipités dépend essentiellement des conditions du milieu environnant, avec peu de contrôle cellulaire (9).

tu.prop: As a result, the chemical compositionof precipitates depends essentially onthe surrounding environmental conditions, withlittle control by the cell (9).

However,cyanobacterial calcification has been studied onlyin a few model species; thus, our knowledgeof the mechanisms involved remains limited to anarrow phylogenetic window.We studied biofilms dominated by cyanobacteriaderived from modern microbialites collectedin the highly alkaline (pH ~8.9) LakeAlchichica, Mexico (13).

tuv.seg: Calcification efficiencyvaries among different species as a functionof extracellular surface properties, includingexopolymer composition (10) or varying physiologicalstates of the cell (11, 12).

L'efficacité de la calcification varie entre les différentes espèces en fonction des propriétés de la surface extracellulaire, notamment de la composition en exopolymère (10) ou des différents états physiologiques de la cellule (11, 12).

tu.prop: Calcification efficiencyvaries among different species as a functionof extracellular surface properties, includingexopolymer composition (10) or varying physiologicalstates of the cell (11, 12).
Previous studies havedetected a large diversity of bacteria and microbialeukaryotes in these samples, plus a minorarchaeal component (14, 15).

tuv.seg: However,cyanobacterial calcification has been studied onlyin a few model species; thus, our knowledgeof the mechanisms involved remains limited to anarrow phylogenetic window.We studied biofilms dominated by cyanobacteriaderived from modern microbialites collectedin the highly alkaline (pH ~8.9) LakeAlchichica, Mexico (13).

Néanmoins, la calcification n’a été étudiée que dans le cas de quelques espèces modèles. Dès lors, nos connaissances des mécanismes impliqués restent limitées à un groupe phylogénétique restreint. Nous avons étudié des biofilms dominés par des cyanobactéries provenant de microbialites modernes recueillies dans la lagune d'Alchichica, au Mexique, qui est fortement alcaline (pH ~8,9) (13).

tu.prop: However,cyanobacterial calcification has been studied onlyin a few model species; thus, our knowledgeof the mechanisms involved remains limited to anarrow phylogenetic window.We studied biofilms dominated by cyanobacteriaderived from modern microbialites collectedin the highly alkaline (pH ~8.9) LakeAlchichica, Mexico (13).
Alchichica microbialitesare mostly composed of hydromagnesite[Mg5(CO3)4(OH)2•4(H2O)] and aragonite (CaCO3).

tuv.seg: Previous studies havedetected a large diversity of bacteria and microbialeukaryotes in these samples, plus a minorarchaeal component (14, 15).
Des études antérieures ont permis de détecter une grande diversité de bactéries et d'eucaryotes microbiens dans ces échantillons, ainsi qu'une composante archéenne mineure (14, 15).

tu.prop: Previous studies havedetected a large diversity of bacteria and microbialeukaryotes in these samples, plus a minorarchaeal component (14, 15).
In the course of long-term studies of Alchichicamicrobialites maintained in laboratory aquaria,we observed the conspicuous development ofphototrophic biofilms on aquarium walls (fig. S1).

tuv.seg: Alchichica microbialitesare mostly composed of hydromagnesite[Mg<ex id="_25"/><g id="26">5</g><g id="27">(CO</g><g id="28">3</g><g id="29">)</g><g id="30">4</g><g id="31">(OH)</g><g id="32">2</g><g id="33">•4(H</g><g id="34">2</g><g id="35">O)] and aragonite (CaCO</g><g id="36">3</g><bx id="37"/>).

Les microbialites de la lagune d'Alchichica sont principalement composées d'hydromagnésite [Mg<ex id="_25"/><g id="26">5</g><g id="27">(CO</g><g id="28">3</g><g id="29">)</g><g id="30">4</g><g id="31">(OH)</g><g id="32">2</g><g id="33">•4(H</g><g id="34">2</g><g id="35">O)] et d'aragonite (CaCO</g><g id="36">3</g><bx id="37"/>).

tu.prop: Alchichica microbialitesare mostly composed of hydromagnesite[Mg5(CO3)4(OH)2•4(H2O)] and aragonite (CaCO3).

The biofilms were dominated by cyanobacteria ofdifferent sizes and morphotypes (13), also recognizableby confocal laser scanning microscopy(CLSM) according to their red autofluorescence(using a green excitation wavelength).

tuv.seg: In the course of long-term studies of Alchichicamicrobialites maintained in laboratory aquaria,we observed the conspicuous development ofphototrophic biofilms on aquarium walls (fig. S1).

Au cours d'études à long terme des microbialites de la lagune d'Alchichica maintenus dans des aquariums, nous avons observé le développement remarquable des biofilms phototrophes sur les parois de l'aquarium (cf. Fig. S1).

tu.prop: In the course of long-term studies of Alchichicamicrobialites maintained in laboratory aquaria,we observed the conspicuous development ofphototrophic biofilms on aquarium walls (fig. S1).
Amongthese different morphotypes, one relatively small,rod-shaped, unicellular morphotype was particularlyabundant (fig. S1).

tuv.seg: The biofilms were dominated by cyanobacteria ofdifferent sizes and morphotypes (13), also recognizableby confocal laser scanning microscopy(CLSM) according to their red autofluorescence(using a green excitation wavelength).

Les biofilms étaient majoritairement composés de cyanobactéries de différentes tailles et morphotypes (13), également identifiables par leur autofluorescence rouge révélée en utilisant de la microscopie confocale à balayage laser (MCBL), à l'aide d'une longueur d'onde d'excitation verte.

tu.prop: The biofilms were dominated by cyanobacteria ofdifferent sizes and morphotypes (13), also recognizableby confocal laser scanning microscopy(CLSM) according to their red autofluorescence(using a green excitation wavelength).
These cells measured3.9 T 0.6 mm in length and 1.1 T 0.1 mm in widthand exhibited a granular cytoplasm (Fig. 1, Aand B, and table S1).

tuv.seg: Amongthese different morphotypes, one relatively small,rod-shaped, unicellular morphotype was particularlyabundant (fig. S1).
Parmi les différents morphotypes, une cyanobactérie unicellulaire, relativement petite et en forme de bâtonnet était particulièrement abondante.

tu.prop: Amongthese different morphotypes, one relatively small,rod-shaped, unicellular morphotype was particularlyabundant (fig. S1).
Autofluorescence emissionspectra measured on individual cells showed thepresence of chlorophyll and phycocyanin, supportingtheir identification as cyanobacteria (fig.S2).

tuv.seg: These cells measured3.9 T 0.6 mm in length and 1.1 T 0.1 mm in widthand exhibited a granular cytoplasm (Fig. 1, Aand B, and table S1).
Ces cellules mesuraient 3,9 ± 0,6 μm de longueur et 1,1 ± 0,1 μm de largeur et présentaient un cytoplasme granulaire (cf. Fig.1, A et B et tableau S1).

tu.prop: These cells measured3.9 T 0.6 mm in length and 1.1 T 0.1 mm in widthand exhibited a granular cytoplasm (Fig. 1, Aand B, and table S1).
Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) revealedspherical intracellular granules in thismorphotype with average diameter of 270 T 44nm (Fig. 1, C to I, and table S1).

tuv.seg: Autofluorescence emissionspectra measured on individual cells showed thepresence of chlorophyll and phycocyanin, supportingtheir identification as cyanobacteria (fig.S2).

Les spectres d’émission d’autofluorescence mesurés individuellement sur chaque cellule montraient la présence de chlorophylle et de phycocyanine, ce qui soutient l’hypothèse selon laquelle il s’agirait de cyanobactéries (cf. Fig. S2).

tu.prop: Autofluorescence emissionspectra measured on individual cells showed thepresence of chlorophyll and phycocyanin, supportingtheir identification as cyanobacteria (fig.S2).
The averagenumber of inclusions per cell was 21 T 5.

tuv.seg: Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) revealedspherical intracellular granules in thismorphotype with average diameter of 270 T 44nm (Fig. 1, C to I, and table S1).

Les analyses obtenues par microscopie électronique à balayage (MEB) ont révélé la présence de granules intracellulaires de forme sphérique à l’intérieur de ce morphotype dont le diamètre moyen s’élevait à 270 ± 44 nm (cf. Fig. 1, de C à I et tableau S1).

tu.prop: Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) revealedspherical intracellular granules in thismorphotype with average diameter of 270 T 44nm (Fig. 1, C to I, and table S1).

Theseinclusions were very bright in backscattered electronand secondary electron modes, suggestingthat they contained elements of high atomic number.Their chemical composition was determinedby energy-dispersive x-ray spectroscopy (EDXS;Fig. 2A).

tuv.seg: The averagenumber of inclusions per cell was 21 T 5.
Le nombre moyen d'inclusions par cellule était de 21 T 5.

tu.prop: The averagenumber of inclusions per cell was 21 T 5.
Inclusions contained Ca, Mg, Ba, and Sras major elements with atomic ratios Ca/Mg =2.88, Ba/Mg = 1.11, and Sr/Mg = 0.42 (table S2).

tuv.seg: Theseinclusions were very bright in backscattered electronand secondary electron modes, suggestingthat they contained elements of high atomic number.Their chemical composition was determinedby energy-dispersive x-ray spectroscopy (EDXS;Fig. 2A).

Ces inclusions étaient très lumineuses dans les modes électron rétrodiffusé et électron secondaire, suggérant qu'elles contenaient des éléments de numéro atomique élevé. Leur composition chimique a été déterminée par spectroscopie de rayons X à dispersion d'énergie (EDXS ; cf. Fig. 2A).

tu.prop: Theseinclusions were very bright in backscattered electronand secondary electron modes, suggestingthat they contained elements of high atomic number.Their chemical composition was determinedby energy-dispersive x-ray spectroscopy (EDXS;Fig. 2A).

These ratios were markedly different from thosemeasured in the solution; relative to the mediumwhere cells grew, Ba/Ca and Sr/Ca atomic ratiosin the inclusions were higher by factors of 1370and 86, respectively (tables S2 and S3).

tuv.seg: Inclusions contained Ca, Mg, Ba, and Sras major elements with atomic ratios Ca/Mg =2.88, Ba/Mg = 1.11, and Sr/Mg = 0.42 (table S2).
Les inclusions étaient composées de Ca, Mg, Ba et Sr comme éléments principaux, avec des rapports atomiques Ca/Mg = 2,88, Ba/Mg = 1,11, et Sr/Mg = 0,42 (cf. tableau S2).

tu.prop: Inclusions contained Ca, Mg, Ba, and Sras major elements with atomic ratios Ca/Mg =2.88, Ba/Mg = 1.11, and Sr/Mg = 0.42 (table S2).
We investigated the chemical compositionand structure of the Ca-Mg-Sr-Ba inclusionswith the use of transmission electron microscopy(TEM) and scanning transmission x-ray microscopy(STXM) (13).

tuv.seg: These ratios were markedly different from thosemeasured in the solution; relative to the mediumwhere cells grew, Ba/Ca and Sr/Ca atomic ratiosin the inclusions were higher by factors of 1370and 86, respectively (tables S2 and S3).

Ces rapports étaient nettement différents de ceux mesurés dans la solution ; par rapport au milieu où les cellules se développaient, les rapports atomiques Ba/Ca et Sr/Ca dans les inclusions étaient plus élevés par des facteurs de 1370 et 86, respectivement (cf. tableaux S2 et S3).

tu.prop: These ratios were markedly different from thosemeasured in the solution; relative to the mediumwhere cells grew, Ba/Ca and Sr/Ca atomic ratiosin the inclusions were higher by factors of 1370and 86, respectively (tables S2 and S3).
STXM allows imagingand acquisition of x-ray absorption near-edgestructure (XANES) spectra at high spectral (~0.1eV) and spatial (~25 nm) resolution.

tuv.seg: We investigated the chemical compositionand structure of the Ca-Mg-Sr-Ba inclusionswith the use of transmission electron microscopy(TEM) and scanning transmission x-ray microscopy(STXM) (13).

Nous avons examiné la composition chimique et la structure des inclusions Ca-Mg-Sr-Ba en utilisant la microscopie électronique en transmission (MET) et la microscopie à balayage par transmission de rayons X (STXM) (13).

tu.prop: We investigated the chemical compositionand structure of the Ca-Mg-Sr-Ba inclusionswith the use of transmission electron microscopy(TEM) and scanning transmission x-ray microscopy(STXM) (13).
A peak at290.3 eV in the XANES spectra measured at theC K-edge on intracellular inclusions (Fig. 2Cand table S4) was attributed unambiguously to1s-p* electronic transitions in carbonate groups(16).

tuv.seg: STXM allows imagingand acquisition of x-ray absorption near-edgestructure (XANES) spectra at high spectral (~0.1eV) and spatial (~25 nm) resolution.
La STXM permet l'imagerie et l'acquisition de spectres XANES (spectroscopie de structure près du seuil d'absorption de rayons X) à haute résolution spectrale (~0,1 eV) et spatiale (~25 nm).

tu.prop: STXM allows imagingand acquisition of x-ray absorption near-edgestructure (XANES) spectra at high spectral (~0.1eV) and spatial (~25 nm) resolution.
Selected-area electron diffraction patternsobtained by TEM on these inclusions suggestedthat they are amorphous (Fig. 2B).

tuv.seg: A peak at290.3 eV in the XANES spectra measured at theC K-edge on intracellular inclusions (Fig. 2Cand table S4) was attributed unambiguously to1s-p* electronic transitions in carbonate groups(16).

Un pic à 290,3 eV dans les spectres XANES, mesuré au seuil K du carbone sur des inclusions intracellulaires (cf. Figure 2C et tableau S4), a été explicitement attribué aux transitions électroniques 1s-p* dans les groupes carbonate (16).

tu.prop: A peak at290.3 eV in the XANES spectra measured at theC K-edge on intracellular inclusions (Fig. 2Cand table S4) was attributed unambiguously to1s-p* electronic transitions in carbonate groups(16).
XANES spectraat the Ca L2,3 edges are indicative of the localstructure around Ca and can be used to characterizepoorly organized phases [e.g., (16, 17)].

tuv.seg: Selected-area electron diffraction patternsobtained by TEM on these inclusions suggestedthat they are amorphous (Fig. 2B).
Le motif de diffraction électronique de la zone sélectionnée obtenu par MET sur ces inclusions suggère qu'elles sont amorphes (cf. Fig. 2B).

tu.prop: Selected-area electron diffraction patternsobtained by TEM on these inclusions suggestedthat they are amorphous (Fig. 2B).

Spectra measured on intracellular inclusions weredifferent from spectra of common reference Ca carbonatessuch as aragonite, calcite, or Ca-substitutedstrontianite, but they were very similar to thespectrum of benstonite, a Mg-Ca-Sr-Ba carbonate(Fig. 2D and table S5).

tuv.seg: XANES spectraat the Ca L<ex id="_37"/><g id="38">2,3 </g><bx id="39"/>edges are indicative of the localstructure around Ca and can be used to characterizepoorly organized phases [e.g., (16, 17)].

Les spectres XANES sur les seuils L<ex id="_37"/><g id="38">2,3</g><bx id="39"/>du calcium donnent une indication de la structure locale autour du Ca et peuvent être utilisés pour caractériser des phases mal organisées [ex, (16, 17)].

tu.prop: XANES spectraat the Ca L2,3 edges are indicative of the localstructure around Ca and can be used to characterizepoorly organized phases [e.g., (16, 17)].
The stoichiometricformula of the intracellular inclusions can thusbe written as (Sr1Ba2.7Mg1.4Ca0.9)Ca6Mg(CO3)13,following that of benstonite and in agreementwith EDXS analyses.

tuv.seg: Spectra measured on intracellular inclusions weredifferent from spectra of common reference Ca carbonatessuch as aragonite, calcite, or Ca-substitutedstrontianite, but they were very similar to thespectrum of benstonite, a Mg-Ca-Sr-Ba carbonate(Fig. 2D and table S5).

Les spectres mesurés sur les inclusions intracellulaires étaient différents des spectres de référence des carbonates de calcium, tels que l’aragonite, la calcite ou encore la strontianite qui est remplacée par le calcium, mais ils étaient très similaires au spectre du bentonite, un carbonate de Mg-Ca-Sr-Ba (cf. Figure 2D et tableau S5).

tu.prop: Spectra measured on intracellular inclusions weredifferent from spectra of common reference Ca carbonatessuch as aragonite, calcite, or Ca-substitutedstrontianite, but they were very similar to thespectrum of benstonite, a Mg-Ca-Sr-Ba carbonate(Fig. 2D and table S5).
Hence, these intracellularinclusions are composed of an unusual benstonitelikephase with no long-range order and an unusualstoichiometry.

tuv.seg: The stoichiometricformula of the intracellular inclusions can thusbe written as (Sr<ex id="_39"/><g id="40">1</g><g id="41">Ba</g><g id="42">2.7</g><g id="43">Mg</g><g id="44">1.4</g><g id="45">Ca</g><g id="46">0.9</g><g id="47">)Ca</g><g id="48">6</g><g id="49">Mg(CO</g><g id="50">3</g><g id="51">)</g><g id="52">13</g><bx id="53"/>,following that of benstonite and in agreementwith EDXS analyses.

La forme stœchiométrique des inclusions intracellulaires peut ainsi s'écrire (Sr<ex id="_39"/><g id="40">1</g><g id="41">Ba</g><g id="42">2,7</g><g id="43">Mg</g><g id="44">1,4</g><g id="45">Ca</g><g id="46">0,9</g><g id="47">)Ca</g><g id="48">6</g><g id="49">Mg(CO</g><g id="50">3</g><g id="51">)</g><g id="52">13</g><bx id="53"/>, suivant celle de la benstonite et en accord avec les analyses EDXS.

tu.prop: The stoichiometricformula of the intracellular inclusions can thusbe written as (Sr1Ba2.7Mg1.4Ca0.9)Ca6Mg(CO3)13,following that of benstonite and in agreementwith EDXS analyses.
In total, inclusions occupied~6% of the total cell volume, raising the totaldensity of the cells by 12% (tables S6 and S7).

tuv.seg: Hence, these intracellularinclusions are composed of an unusual benstonitelikephase with no long-range order and an unusualstoichiometry.
Par conséquent, les inclusions intracellulaires comportent une phase inhabituelle qui s’apparente à celle de la bentonite, sans ordre à longue distance et présentant une stœchiométrie singulière.

tu.prop: Hence, these intracellularinclusions are composed of an unusual benstonitelikephase with no long-range order and an unusualstoichiometry.

We enriched these cyanobacteria in cultureby inoculating modified BG11 medium withcells smaller than 3 mm from disrupted biofilms.CLSM and SEM observations showed that sixenrichment cultures (out of 96) contained thissingle cyanobacterial morphotype with intracellularinclusions—that is, with no additional cyanobacterialmorphotype as detected by microscopy (fig. S3).

tuv.seg: In total, inclusions occupied~6% of the total cell volume, raising the totaldensity of the cells by 12% (tables S6 and S7).
Au total, les inclusions occupaient ~6 % du volume total de la cellule, ce qui a augmenté la densité totale des cellules de 12 % (cf. tableaux S6 et S7).

tu.prop: In total, inclusions occupied~6% of the total cell volume, raising the totaldensity of the cells by 12% (tables S6 and S7).

This was further confirmed by amplificationand sequencing of 16S ribosomal RNA(rRNA) genes using cyanobacterial-specific primers,which provided 100% identical sequenceswithin and among the six enrichments (table S8).

tuv.seg: We enriched these cyanobacteria in cultureby inoculating modified BG11 medium withcells smaller than 3 mm from disrupted biofilms.CLSM and SEM observations showed that sixenrichment cultures (out of 96) contained thissingle cyanobacterial morphotype with intracellularinclusions—that is, with no additional cyanobacterialmorphotype as detected by microscopy (fig. S3).

Nous avons mis en culture les cyanobactéries en inoculant du BG11 modifié avec des cellules inférieures à 3 μm à partir d’un arrachement de biofilms. Les observations au microscope confocal à balayage laser (MCBL) et au microscope électronique à balayage (MEB) ont montré que six cultures d’enrichissement (sur 96) contenaient cet unique morphotype de cyanobactérie avec des inclusions intracellulaires, c’est-à-dire avec aucun morphotype de cyanobactérie supplémentaire détecté par le microscope (cf. Fig. S3).

tu.prop: We enriched these cyanobacteria in cultureby inoculating modified BG11 medium withcells smaller than 3 mm from disrupted biofilms.CLSM and SEM observations showed that sixenrichment cultures (out of 96) contained thissingle cyanobacterial morphotype with intracellularinclusions—that is, with no additional cyanobacterialmorphotype as detected by microscopy (fig. S3).
Phylogenetic analysis showed that this strainwas a member of the basal cyanobacterial orderGloeobacterales (Fig. 3).

tuv.seg: This was further confirmed by amplificationand sequencing of 16S ribosomal RNA(rRNA) genes using cyanobacterial-specific primers,which provided 100% identical sequenceswithin and among the six enrichments (table S8).

Cela a en outre été confirmé par l'amplification et le séquençage des gènes de l'ARN ribosomique 16S (ARNr) à l'aide d'amorces spécifiques aux cyanobactéries, qui ont fourni des séquences 100 % identiques dans et parmi les six enrichissements (cf. tableau S8).

tu.prop: This was further confirmed by amplificationand sequencing of 16S ribosomal RNA(rRNA) genes using cyanobacterial-specific primers,which provided 100% identical sequenceswithin and among the six enrichments (table S8).

The sequences obtained from the enrichmentcultures were almost identical (99.7% sequenceidentity) to AQ1_1_1C_35 (CyanoOTU02) (13),an environmental sequence abundantly retrievedfrom aquarium microbialites (up to 20% of cyanobacterialsequences) (15).

tuv.seg: Phylogenetic analysis showed that this strainwas a member of the basal cyanobacterial orderGloeobacterales (Fig. 3).
L’analyse phylogénétique a démontré que cette souche faisait partie de l’ordre basal des cyanobactéries Gloeobacterales (cf. Fig. 3).

tu.prop: Phylogenetic analysis showed that this strainwas a member of the basal cyanobacterial orderGloeobacterales (Fig. 3).
This indicates thatthis lineage is an important cyanobacterial componentof actively growing microbialites, beingable to disperse and form neighboring biofilmson aquarium walls.

tuv.seg: The sequences obtained from the enrichmentcultures were almost identical (99.7% sequenceidentity) to AQ1_1_1C_35 (CyanoOTU02) (13),an environmental sequence abundantly retrievedfrom aquarium microbialites (up to 20% of cyanobacterialsequences) (15).

Les séquences obtenues à partir des cultures d'enrichissement étaient presque identiques (99,7 % d'identité de séquence) à AQ1_1_1C_35 (CyanoOTU02) (13), une séquence environnementale extraite en grande quantité des microbialites d'aquarium (jusqu'à 20 % de séquences cyanobactériennes) (15).

tu.prop: The sequences obtained from the enrichmentcultures were almost identical (99.7% sequenceidentity) to AQ1_1_1C_35 (CyanoOTU02) (13),an environmental sequence abundantly retrievedfrom aquarium microbialites (up to 20% of cyanobacterialsequences) (15).

Because it is phylogeneticallyvery distant from Gloeobacter, the onlygenus described within Gloeobacterales, we proposethe following status: order Gloeobacterales,Candidatus Gloeomargarita lithophora gen. et sp.nov.

tuv.seg: This indicates thatthis lineage is an important cyanobacterial componentof actively growing microbialites, beingable to disperse and form neighboring biofilmson aquarium walls.

Ceci indique que cette lignée est un composant de cyanobactérie significatif des microbialites en développement rapide, étant à même de se disperser et de former des biofilms proches sur les parois des aquariums.

tu.prop: This indicates thatthis lineage is an important cyanobacterial componentof actively growing microbialites, beingable to disperse and form neighboring biofilmson aquarium walls.

tuv.seg: Because it is phylogeneticallyvery distant from Gloeobacter, the onlygenus described within Gloeobacterales, we proposethe following status: order Gloeobacterales,Candidatus Gloeomargarita lithophora gen. et sp.nov.

Parce qu’elle est phylogénétiquement très éloignée de Gloeobacter, le seul genre décrit au sein de Gloeobacterales, nous proposons le statut suivant : ordre des Gloeobacterales, Candidatus Gloeomargarita lithophora gen. et sp. nov.

tu.prop: Because it is phylogeneticallyvery distant from Gloeobacter, the onlygenus described within Gloeobacterales, we proposethe following status: order Gloeobacterales,Candidatus Gloeomargarita lithophora gen. et sp.nov.

The amorphous intracellular bioprecipitatesare structurally and chemically different fromthe well-crystallized phases forming in the extracellularsolution (i.e., aragonite and hydromagnesite)(tables S2 and S3).
Incorporation ofSr and Ba in carbonates has implications forSr/Ca and Ba/Ca ratios in marine carbonates,which are frequently used as paleoenvironmentalproxies (18).

tuv.seg: The amorphous intracellular bioprecipitatesare structurally and chemically different fromthe well-crystallized phases forming in the extracellularsolution (i.e., aragonite and hydromagnesite)(tables S2 and S3).

Les bioprécipités intracellulaires amorphes sont structurellement et chimiquement différents des phases bien cristallisées se formant dans la solution extracellulaire (c'est-à-dire l'aragonite et l'hydromagnésite) (cf. tableaux S2 et S3).

tu.prop: The amorphous intracellular bioprecipitatesare structurally and chemically different fromthe well-crystallized phases forming in the extracellularsolution (i.e., aragonite and hydromagnesite)(tables S2 and S3).

Such proxies rely on the assumptionthat these ratios are approximately the same (orslightly lower) in the solid phases as in the solution.In Candidatus G. lithophora intracellularinclusions, these ratios are much higher in thesolid phase and are similar to what has beenfound in some algae (19).

tuv.seg: Incorporation ofSr and Ba in carbonates has implications forSr/Ca and Ba/Ca ratios in marine carbonates,which are frequently used as paleoenvironmentalproxies (18).

L’incorporation de Sr et de Ba dans les carbonates a des conséquences pour les rapports atomiques Sr/Ca et Ba/Ca au sein des carbonates marins, qui sont généralement utilisés comme des proxys paléoenvironnementaux (18).

tu.prop: Incorporation ofSr and Ba in carbonates has implications forSr/Ca and Ba/Ca ratios in marine carbonates,which are frequently used as paleoenvironmentalproxies (18).
Such an enrichment inSr and Ba might involve active import systemsspecific for Sr2+ and Ba2+ and/or export systemsextruding specifically Ca2+ and maintaining lowcytoplasmic Ca2+ levels (20).

tuv.seg: Such proxies rely on the assumptionthat these ratios are approximately the same (orslightly lower) in the solid phases as in the solution.In Candidatus G. lithophora intracellularinclusions, these ratios are much higher in thesolid phase and are similar to what has beenfound in some algae (19).

De tels proxies reposent sur l'hypothèse que ces rapports sont approximativement les mêmes (ou légèrement inférieurs) dans les phases solides que dans la solution. Dans les inclusions intracellulaires de Candidatus G. lithophora, ces rapports sont bien plus élevés dans la phase solide et sont similaires à ce qui a été trouvé dans certaines algues (19).

tu.prop: Such proxies rely on the assumptionthat these ratios are approximately the same (orslightly lower) in the solid phases as in the solution.In Candidatus G. lithophora intracellularinclusions, these ratios are much higher in thesolid phase and are similar to what has beenfound in some algae (19).
The amorphousstructure of the inclusions is somewhat similar torelatively unstable amorphous calcium carbonates(ACCs) observed in biological, mostly eukaryoticsystems (21).

tuv.seg: Such an enrichment inSr and Ba might involve active import systemsspecific for Sr<ex id="_53"/><g id="54">2+ </g><g id="55">and Ba</g><g id="56">2+ </g><g id="57">and/or export systemsextruding specifically Ca</g><g id="58">2+ </g><g id="59">and maintaining lowcytoplasmic Ca</g><g id="60">2+ </g><bx id="61"/>levels (20).

Un tel enrichissement en Sr et Ba pourrait impliquer un système d'importation actif spécifique au Sr<ex id="_53"/>2+<g id="54"></g><g id="55">et au Ba</g><g id="56">2+ </g><g id="57">et/ou un système d'exportation extrudant spécifiquement le Ca</g><g id="58">2+ </g><g id="59">et maintenant de faibles niveaux de Ca</g><g id="60">2+ </g><bx id="61"/>cytoplasmique (20).

tu.prop: Such an enrichment inSr and Ba might involve active import systemsspecific for Sr2+ and Ba2+ and/or export systemsextruding specifically Ca2+ and maintaining lowcytoplasmic Ca2+ levels (20).
Consistent with what has oftenbeen observed in these ACC-containing systems(21), the presence of organic macromolecules mayFig.

tuv.seg: The amorphousstructure of the inclusions is somewhat similar torelatively unstable amorphous calcium carbonates(ACCs) observed in biological, mostly eukaryoticsystems (21).
La structure amorphe des inclusions est assez similaire aux carbonates de calcium amorphes (ACC) relativement instables observés dans les systèmes biologiques, principalement eucaryotes (21).

tu.prop: The amorphousstructure of the inclusions is somewhat similar torelatively unstable amorphous calcium carbonates(ACCs) observed in biological, mostly eukaryoticsystems (21).

tuv.seg: Consistent with what has oftenbeen observed in these ACC-containing systems(21), the presence of organic macromolecules mayFig.

La présence de macromolécules organiques peut permettre de stabiliser les carbonates amorphes intracellulaires observés dans la Candidatus G. lithophora, concordant avec ce qui a été observé dans ces systèmes contenant des ACC (21).

tu.prop: Consistent with what has oftenbeen observed in these ACC-containing systems(21), the presence of organic macromolecules mayFig.
Visualization of cyanobacterial cells forming intracellular mineralinclusions.
(A) Composite epifluorescence and optical microscopy imagesshowing rod-shaped autofluorescent cyanobacteria cells.

tuv.seg: Visualization of cyanobacterial cells forming intracellular mineralinclusions.
Visualisation des cellules de cyanobactéries formant des inclusions minérales intracellulaires.

tu.prop: Visualization of cyanobacterial cells forming intracellular mineralinclusions.
(B) Optical imageshows intracellular inclusions in cyanobacterial cells (higher magnification ininset).

tuv.seg: (A) Composite epifluorescence and optical microscopy imagesshowing rod-shaped autofluorescent cyanobacteria cells.
(A) Images composites affichant des cellules de cyanobactéries autofluorescentes en forme de bâtonnet, obtenues en utilisant la microscopie à épifluorescence et la microscopie optique.

tu.prop: (A) Composite epifluorescence and optical microscopy imagesshowing rod-shaped autofluorescent cyanobacteria cells.
(C to F) SEM images (secondary electron mode) showing cells of thesame morphotype, which systematically contain bright intracellular inclusions.(

tuv.seg: (B) Optical imageshows intracellular inclusions in cyanobacterial cells (higher magnification ininset).
(B) L'image optique révèle des inclusions intracellulaires dans des cellules de cyanobactéries (plus fort grossissement dans l'encadré)

tu.prop: (B) Optical imageshows intracellular inclusions in cyanobacterial cells (higher magnification ininset).
G to I) CSLM (G), phase contrast (H), and SEM (I) images of exactlythe same area, showing that autofluorescent cells observed by CLSM containthe bright inclusions observed by SEM (inset).

tuv.seg: (C to F) SEM images (secondary electron mode) showing cells of thesame morphotype, which systematically contain bright intracellular inclusions.(
(C à F) Images MEB (mode électron secondaire) montrant des cellules du même morphotype, qui contiennent systématiquement des inclusions intracellulaires brillantes. (

tu.prop: (C to F) SEM images (secondary electron mode) showing cells of thesame morphotype, which systematically contain bright intracellular inclusions.(

A different, larger, andinclusion-deprived cyanobacterial morphotype appears on the right.460 27 APRIL 2012 VOL 336 SCIENCE www.sciencemag.orgREPORTSstabilize the intracellular amorphous carbonatesobserved in Candidatus G. lithophora.

tuv.seg: G to I) CSLM (G), phase contrast (H), and SEM (I) images of exactlythe same area, showing that autofluorescent cells observed by CLSM containthe bright inclusions observed by SEM (inset).

(G à I) MCBL (G), contraste de phase (H) et images MEB (I) d'exactement la même zone, montrant que les cellules autofluorescentes observées par MCBL contiennent les inclusions brillantes observées par MEB (encart).

tu.prop: G to I) CSLM (G), phase contrast (H), and SEM (I) images of exactlythe same area, showing that autofluorescent cells observed by CLSM containthe bright inclusions observed by SEM (inset).
Diverselocal structures were recently suggested for thesephases lacking long-range order (22).

tuv.seg: A different, larger, andinclusion-deprived cyanobacterial morphotype appears on the right.<ex id="_61"/><g id="62">460 </g><g id="63">27 APRIL 2012 VOL 336 SCIENCE www.sciencemag.org</g><g id="64">REPORTS</g><bx id="65"/>stabilize the intracellular amorphous carbonatesobserved in Candidatus G. lithophora.

Un morphotype cyanobactérien différent, plus grand et privé d'inclusion apparaît à droite.<ex id="_61"/><g id="62">460 </g><g id="63">27 AVRIL 2012 VOL 336 SCIENCE www.sciencemag.org</g><g id="64">REPORTS</g><bx id="65"/> stabiliser les carbonates amorphes intracellulaires observés chez Candidatus G. lithophora.

tu.prop: A different, larger, andinclusion-deprived cyanobacterial morphotype appears on the right.460 27 APRIL 2012 VOL 336 SCIENCE www.sciencemag.orgREPORTSstabilize the intracellular amorphous carbonatesobserved in Candidatus G. lithophora.
The Mg-,Ca-, Sr-, and Ba-containing inclusions expand theknown diversity of ACC compositions.Intracellular carbonate precipitation in thiscyanobacterium likely influences its biologyand ecology.

tuv.seg: Diverselocal structures were recently suggested for thesephases lacking long-range order (22).
De diverses structures locales ont été récemment suggérées pour ces phases manquant d'ordre à longue portée (22).

tu.prop: Diverselocal structures were recently suggested for thesephases lacking long-range order (22).

First, by changing average cell density,these precipitates must alter cell buoyancy.The conspicuous occurrence of CandidatusG. lithophora in aquarium wall biofilms impliesthat a dispersive planktonic phase existsthat allows the colonization of surrounding surfaces.Therefore, whereas gas vesicles functionas flotation devices in planktonic cyanobacteriaand other prokaryotes (23), intracellular carbonatesmight serve as ballast—an adaptation to abenthic mode of life.

tuv.seg: The Mg-,Ca-, Sr-, and Ba-containing inclusions expand theknown diversity of ACC compositions.Intracellular carbonate precipitation in thiscyanobacterium likely influences its biologyand ecology.

Les inclusions contenant du Mg, du Ca, du Sr et du Ba élargissent la diversité connue des compositions des ACC. La précipitation de carbonate intracellulaire chez cette cyanobactérie influence probablement sa biologie et son écologie.

tu.prop: The Mg-,Ca-, Sr-, and Ba-containing inclusions expand theknown diversity of ACC compositions.Intracellular carbonate precipitation in thiscyanobacterium likely influences its biologyand ecology.

Second, the formation ofintracellular carbonates implies that the alkalinityexcess produced by cyanobacteria duringphotosynthetic carbon fixation, a process takingplace in carboxysomes, is not always exportedextracellularly but can be trapped withinamorphous carbonate inclusions.

tuv.seg: First, by changing average cell density,these precipitates must alter cell buoyancy.The conspicuous occurrence of CandidatusG. lithophora in aquarium wall biofilms impliesthat a dispersive planktonic phase existsthat allows the colonization of surrounding surfaces.Therefore, whereas gas vesicles functionas flotation devices in planktonic cyanobacteriaand other prokaryotes (23), intracellular carbonatesmight serve as ballast—an adaptation to abenthic mode of life.

Tout d'abord, en modifiant la densité cellulaire moyenne, ces précipités doivent altérer la flottabilité cellulaire. L'apparition ostensible de Candidatus G. lithophora dans les biofilms des parois des aquariums implique l'existence d'une phase planctonique dispersive qui permet la colonisation des surfaces environnantes. Par conséquent, alors que les vésicules de gaz fonctionnent comme des dispositifs de flottaison chez les cyanobactéries planctoniques et d'autres procaryotes (23), les carbonates intracellulaires pourraient servir de ballast - une adaptation au mode de vie abenthique.

tu.prop: First, by changing average cell density,these precipitates must alter cell buoyancy.The conspicuous occurrence of CandidatusG. lithophora in aquarium wall biofilms impliesthat a dispersive planktonic phase existsthat allows the colonization of surrounding surfaces.Therefore, whereas gas vesicles functionas flotation devices in planktonic cyanobacteriaand other prokaryotes (23), intracellular carbonatesmight serve as ballast—an adaptation to abenthic mode of life.
Whether carboxysomescould also provide nucleation centersfor carbonate precipitation remains an openquestion.

tuv.seg: Second, the formation ofintracellular carbonates implies that the alkalinityexcess produced by cyanobacteria duringphotosynthetic carbon fixation, a process takingplace in carboxysomes, is not always exportedextracellularly but can be trapped withinamorphous carbonate inclusions.

Deuxièmement, la formation de carbonates intracellulaires implique que l'excès d'alcalinité produit par les cyanobactéries pendant la fixation du carbone photosynthétique, un processus qui se déroule dans les carboxysomes, n'est pas toujours exporté de manière extracellulaire mais peut être piégé dans des inclusions carbonatées amorphes.

tu.prop: Second, the formation ofintracellular carbonates implies that the alkalinityexcess produced by cyanobacteria duringphotosynthetic carbon fixation, a process takingplace in carboxysomes, is not always exportedextracellularly but can be trapped withinamorphous carbonate inclusions.
Carbonate precipitation could act asa pH buffering system leading to an efficientcarbon-concentrating mechanism and hence ahigh photosynthesis rate (3).

tuv.seg: Whether carboxysomescould also provide nucleation centersfor carbonate precipitation remains an openquestion.
La question de savoir si les carboxysomes pourraient également constituer des centres de nucléation pour la précipitation des carbonates demeure.

tu.prop: Whether carboxysomescould also provide nucleation centersfor carbonate precipitation remains an openquestion.

Because intracellular biomineralization occursin a phylogenetically basal lineage of cyanobacteriathat has retained ancestral characteristics(24) and because the alkalinity export mayrequire the evolution of specific machinery, itmight be argued that this calcification modewas performed by some ancestral cyanobacteria.Gloeobacterales is the only cyanobacterial orderbranching out before the chloroplast clade inmolecular phylogenies of conserved gene markers(Fig. 3) (25).

tuv.seg: Carbonate precipitation could act asa pH buffering system leading to an efficientcarbon-concentrating mechanism and hence ahigh photosynthesis rate (3).
La précipitation de carbonates pourrait agir comme un système tampon de pH conduisant à un mécanisme efficace de concentration de carbone et donc à un taux de photosynthèse élevé (3).

tu.prop: Carbonate precipitation could act asa pH buffering system leading to an efficientcarbon-concentrating mechanism and hence ahigh photosynthesis rate (3).

Not only Gloeobacter violaceus,isolated from limestone biofilms (24), but alsomost environmental sequences forming the largeGloeobacterales clade have been retrieved fromrock-associated biofilms, stromatolites, or thermophilicmicrobial mats (Fig. 3).

tuv.seg: Because intracellular biomineralization occursin a phylogenetically basal lineage of cyanobacteriathat has retained ancestral characteristics(24) and because the alkalinity export mayrequire the evolution of specific machinery, itmight be argued that this calcification modewas performed by some ancestral cyanobacteria.Gloeobacterales is the only cyanobacterial orderbranching out before the chloroplast clade inmolecular phylogenies of conserved gene markers(Fig. 3) (25).

Parce que la biominéralisation intracellulaire se produit dans une lignée phylogénétiquement basale de cyanobactéries qui ont conservé des caractéristiques ancestrales (24) et parce que l'exportation de l'alcalinité peut nécessiter l'évolution de machines spécifiques, on pourrait faire valoir que ce mode de calcification a été effectué par certaines cyanobactéries ancestrales. Les Gloeobacterales sont le seul ordre cyanobactérien qui se ramifie avant les phylogénies moléculaires du clade chloroplastique des marqueurs génétiques conservés (cf. Fig. 3) (25).

tu.prop: Because intracellular biomineralization occursin a phylogenetically basal lineage of cyanobacteriathat has retained ancestral characteristics(24) and because the alkalinity export mayrequire the evolution of specific machinery, itmight be argued that this calcification modewas performed by some ancestral cyanobacteria.Gloeobacterales is the only cyanobacterial orderbranching out before the chloroplast clade inmolecular phylogenies of conserved gene markers(Fig. 3) (25).

This associationindicates a preference for these types of substratesand suggests that Gloeobacterales ancestors mayhave also exhibited the capacity to form biofilmsin moderately hot and/or calcifying environments.Intracellularly biomineralizing cyanobacteriawould not be prone to the formationof microfossils, in contrast to cyanobacteria inducingextracellular precipitation of carbonateswithin sheaths (3, 6).

tuv.seg: Not only Gloeobacter violaceus,isolated from limestone biofilms (24), but alsomost environmental sequences forming the largeGloeobacterales clade have been retrieved fromrock-associated biofilms, stromatolites, or thermophilicmicrobial mats (Fig. 3).

En plus des Gloeobacter violaceus, isolés des biofilms calcaires (24), la plupart des séquences environnementales formant le grand clade des Gloeobacterales a été extraite de biofilms se développant sur des roches, de stromatolites ou de tapis microbiens thermophiles (cf. Fig. 3).

tu.prop: Not only Gloeobacter violaceus,isolated from limestone biofilms (24), but alsomost environmental sequences forming the largeGloeobacterales clade have been retrieved fromrock-associated biofilms, stromatolites, or thermophilicmicrobial mats (Fig. 3).

As a result, microbialitesdominated by intracellularly biomineralizing cyanobacteriamight be devoid of microfossils.Alternatively, if they can be discriminated fromabiotically formed phases (26), traces of thisFig.

tuv.seg: This associationindicates a preference for these types of substratesand suggests that Gloeobacterales ancestors mayhave also exhibited the capacity to form biofilmsin moderately hot and/or calcifying environments.Intracellularly biomineralizing cyanobacteriawould not be prone to the formationof microfossils, in contrast to cyanobacteria inducingextracellular precipitation of carbonateswithin sheaths (3, 6).

Cette association indique une préférence pour ces types de substrats et suggère que les ancêtres des Gloeobacterales auraient eux aussi présenté la capacité de former des biofilms dans des environnements modérément chauds et/ou calcifiants. Les cyanobactéries à biominéralisation intracellulaire ne seraient pas sujettes à la formation de microfossiles, contrairement aux cyanobactéries induisant une précipitation extracellulaire des carbonates dans les gaines (3, 6).

tu.prop: This associationindicates a preference for these types of substratesand suggests that Gloeobacterales ancestors mayhave also exhibited the capacity to form biofilmsin moderately hot and/or calcifying environments.Intracellularly biomineralizing cyanobacteriawould not be prone to the formationof microfossils, in contrast to cyanobacteria inducingextracellular precipitation of carbonateswithin sheaths (3, 6).

tuv.seg: As a result, microbialitesdominated by intracellularly biomineralizing cyanobacteriamight be devoid of microfossils.Alternatively, if they can be discriminated fromabiotically formed phases (26), traces of thisFig.

En conséquence, les microbialites dominés par des cyanobactéries de biominéralisation intracellulaire pourraient être dépourvus de microfossiles. Alternativement, s'ils peuvent être discriminés des phases formées de manière abiotique (26), des traces de cette

tu.prop: As a result, microbialitesdominated by intracellularly biomineralizing cyanobacteriamight be devoid of microfossils.Alternatively, if they can be discriminated fromabiotically formed phases (26), traces of thisFig.
Chemical and mineralogical characterizationof intracellular inclusions.

tuv.seg: Fig. 2.

tu.prop: (A) SEM-EDXS spectrumand associated chemical maps obtained on inclusions(scale bars, 300 nm).

tuv.seg: Chemical and mineralogical characterizationof intracellular inclusions.
Caractérisation chimique et minéralogique des inclusions intracellulaires.

tu.prop: Chemical and mineralogical characterizationof intracellular inclusions.
Only Ca, Mg, Sr, and Baare specifically associated with the inclusions,whereas S is distributed over the whole cells.

tuv.seg: (A) SEM-EDXS spectrumand associated chemical maps obtained on inclusions(scale bars, 300 nm).
(A) Spectre MEB-EDXS et cartes chimiques associées obtenues sur des inclusions (barres d'échelle, 300 nm).

tu.prop: (A) SEM-EDXS spectrumand associated chemical maps obtained on inclusions(scale bars, 300 nm).
(B)Selected-area electron diffraction pattern of an intracellularinclusion (inset; scale bar, 300 nm) showing abroad ring characteristic of an amorphous phase.

tuv.seg: Only Ca, Mg, Sr, and Baare specifically associated with the inclusions,whereas S is distributed over the whole cells.
Seuls le Ca, le Mg, le Sr et le Ba sont spécifiquement associés aux inclusions, tandis que le S est distribué sur l'ensemble des cellules.

tu.prop: Only Ca, Mg, Sr, and Baare specifically associated with the inclusions,whereas S is distributed over the whole cells.
(C)Average STXM-based XANES spectra with associatedmap (inset) measured at the C K-edge on areasshowing only cellular material (yellow) and areascontaining inclusions (blue).

tuv.seg: (B)Selected-area electron diffraction pattern of an intracellularinclusion (inset; scale bar, 300 nm) showing abroad ring characteristic of an amorphous phase.

(B) Les détails de la structure d'une inclusion intracellulaire obtenus par diffraction électronique avec sélection d’aire (voir l'encadré ; barre d'échelle : 300 mm) montrent un large cercle qui est une caractéristique de la phase amorphe.

tu.prop: (B)Selected-area electron diffraction pattern of an intracellularinclusion (inset; scale bar, 300 nm) showing abroad ring characteristic of an amorphous phase.
Spectra are shifted withrespect to each other along the y axis for clarity; eachspectrum has a different origin in y. Peak labelsare reported in table S4.

tuv.seg: (C)Average STXM-based XANES spectra with associatedmap (inset) measured at the C K-edge on areasshowing only cellular material (yellow) and areascontaining inclusions (blue).
(C) Spectres XANES moyens basés sur STXM avec carte associée (voir l'encart) mesurés au bord C K sur le seul matériau cellulaire (en jaune) et les zones contenant des inclusions (en bleu).

tu.prop: (C)Average STXM-based XANES spectra with associatedmap (inset) measured at the C K-edge on areasshowing only cellular material (yellow) and areascontaining inclusions (blue).
(D) STXM-based XANESspectrum measured at the Ca L2,3-edges on intracellularinclusions.

tuv.seg: Spectra are shifted withrespect to each other along the y axis for clarity; eachspectrum has a different origin in y. Peak labelsare reported in table S4.
Les spectres ont été décalés le long de l'axe y pour faciliter la lecture. Chaque spectre a une origine différente sur l'axe y. Les étiquettes de crête sont rapportées dans le tableau S4.

tu.prop: Spectra are shifted withrespect to each other along the y axis for clarity; eachspectrum has a different origin in y. Peak labelsare reported in table S4.
This spectrum is compared withspectra of reference calcite, aragonite, strontianite,and benstonite.

tuv.seg: (D) STXM-based XANESspectrum measured at the Ca L<ex id="_65"/><g id="66">2,3</g><bx id="67"/>-edges on intracellularinclusions.
(D) Spectre XANES à base de STXM mesuré aux bords Ca L<ex id="_65"/><g id="66">2,3</g><bx id="67"/> sur les inclusions intracellulaires.

tu.prop: (D) STXM-based XANESspectrum measured at the Ca L2,3-edges on intracellularinclusions.
The XANES spectrum of the intracellularinclusions matches that of benstonite in peakenergy position and relative peak intensities.

tuv.seg: This spectrum is compared withspectra of reference calcite, aragonite, strontianite,and benstonite.
Ce spectre est comparé avec les spectres de référence de la calcite, l'aragonite, la strontianite et la bentonite.

tu.prop: This spectrum is compared withspectra of reference calcite, aragonite, strontianite,and benstonite.
Peakpositions are reported in table S5.www.sciencemag.org SCIENCE VOL 336 27 APRIL 2012 461REPORTSbiomineralization activity may be sought in theform of Ba- and Sr-rich carbonates.

tuv.seg: The XANES spectrum of the intracellularinclusions matches that of benstonite in peakenergy position and relative peak intensities.
Le spectre XANES des inclusions intracellulaires correspond à celui de la bentonite dans la position en énergie du pic et aux intensités relatives du pic.

tu.prop: The XANES spectrum of the intracellularinclusions matches that of benstonite in peakenergy position and relative peak intensities.
These Ba- andSr-rich phases therefore add to the list of potentialmineral biosignatures in the fossil record.

tuv.seg: Peakpositions are reported in table S5.www.sciencemag.org SCIENCE VOL 336 27 APRIL 2012 <ex id="_67"/><g id="68">461</g><g id="69">REPORTS</g><bx id="70"/>biomineralization activity may be sought in theform of Ba- and Sr-rich carbonates.

Les positions de crête sont rapportées dans le tableau S5. www.sciencemag.org SCIENCE VOL 336 27 AVRIL 2012 <ex id="_67"/><g id="68">461</g><g id="69">REPORTS</g><bx id="70"/>L'activité de biominéralisation peut être recherchée sous la forme de carbonates riches en Ba et en Sr.

tu.prop: Peakpositions are reported in table S5.www.sciencemag.org SCIENCE VOL 336 27 APRIL 2012 461REPORTSbiomineralization activity may be sought in theform of Ba- and Sr-rich carbonates.

tuv.seg: These Ba- andSr-rich phases therefore add to the list of potentialmineral biosignatures in the fossil record.<ex id="_70"/>
En conséquence, ces phases riches en Ba et en Sr doivent être ajoutées à la liste de potentielles biosignatures minérales du registre fossile.<ex id="_70"/>
